data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0914 GMT - Antofagasta and Anglo American fall as LME three-month copper prices slide 1% to $9,854.0 a metric ton on holiday-thinned trading. In recent weeks, prices have risen on month on persistent U.S. tariff uncertainties. Antofagasta slips 2.1% to 1,893 pence but remains up 3.6% on week and 34% over the past three months. Anglo American falls 2% to 2,198 pence. It remains up 2.2% on week and 22% over the past three months. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0901 GMT - Italy's UniCredit will be able to focus on its pursuit of Commerzbank as it looks likely to withdraw its bid for domestic peer Banco BPM, AlphaValue says in a research note. UniCredit's CEO wrote to the German Chancellor about the future of Commerzbank. The probability of a bid on Commerzbank is low but is the best case scenario as it could boost UniCredit's value by up to 15%, analyst David Grinsztajn says. A somewhat likely alternative sees it remaining a strategic shareholder with a positive but more modest valuation impact of less than 5%. The analyst also considers a merger of equals between Commerzbank and UniCredit's German subsidiary HVB, which would seem politically correct but suboptimal in terms of capital and quite improbable. A withdrawal is highly probable and would have no valuation impact, he adds. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0848 GMT - Chesnara's latest deal materially enhances the longer-term sustainability of its dividend growth, Berenberg says in a research note after the insurer bought HSBC's life operations in the U.K. for 260 million pounds. "This is a significant positive for the investment thesis in our view," analyst Michael Huttner writes. The group said that the key attraction of the transaction is that 80% of its 800 million pound total future cash flow expected from the tie-up will be realized after the fifth year, he says. This includes a conservative assumption for cost synergies and the ratio of cash to deal value is over 3 times, which is a very attractive multiple, he writes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0844 GMT - Sterling faces further losses as markets are yet to fully factor in U.K. fiscal risks, Monex Europe analyst say in a note. Sterling fell after Prime Minister Keir Starmer failed to initially back Treasury chief Rachel Reeves in parliament Wednesday. U.K. politics could garner more attention Friday given the lack of market catalysts elsewhere in the world, the analysts say. "Indeed, reports overnight that former Labour leader Jeremy Corbyn is setting up a new political party certainly caught our eye." A challenger to the left of Labour would add pressure on Starmer, they say. Sterling rises 0.1% to $1.3660 but falls against the euro, which is up 0.1% at 0.8614 pounds. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0840 GMT - The latest data shows the London market continues to be sluggish, but the public-equity market might be reaching an inflection point, White & Case Capital Markets analyst Jonathan Parry says in a note. London's nine initial public offerings have raised only 182.8 million pounds in the first half, a fall compared with the 526.7 million pounds raised in eight IPOs in the same period of the prior year, Parry says. Still, the concerted efforts of the London Stock Exchange and the Financial Conduct Authority to reform the regulatory framework are starting to generate momentum, he says. "As a result of these reforms, the London market is now on a level regulatory playing field with other leading listing venues and may be approaching an inflection point that we hope will kickstart a new IPO cycle," he says.(anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0804 GMT - The battle over China's food-delivery market is unlikely to end any time soon, Goldman Sachs analysts say. The intensity of competition between e-commerce players who have entered the space and local services leader Meituan in food delivery and instant shopping has reached new heights, they write in a note. "Unlike previous investments by e-commerce players that were scaled back within a few quarters, we believe the duration of investments this time will extend for longer- given all of the companies are shifting focus to become China's 'everyday app' for transactions across goods and services." Retail players' aim is likely to use food-delivery in cross-selling to their e-commerce offerings, they add.(tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0748 GMT - Samsung Electronics could post a 2Q earnings miss largely due to likely weaker-than-expected semiconductor business, Hana Securities analysts Roko Kim and Y.G. Kim say in a note. They expect the South Korean tech giant's 2Q operating profit to come in at 5.8 trillion won, down 12% from their previous estimate of 6.6 trillion won, after revising down their profit forecast for the memory-chip segment. That would miss the latest FactSet-compiled consensus forecast of 6.5 trillion won. Softer-than-expected shipments of high-bandwidth-memory products likely weighed on the company's DRAM segment, while NAND chip prices may have fallen at a sharper-than-expected pace, they say. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0738 GMT - Thailand's retail sector 2Q same-store sales likely contracted on some headwinds, CGS International's Chaiyatorn Sricharoen says in a research report. Domestic consumption notably deteriorated from April, on factors including falling tourist arrivals and worries over potential U.S. tariff increases, the analyst says. A high base from last year's El Nino-driven sales also weighed on grocery and home improvement segments. However, CGS International maintains the sector's neutral rating as most of the consumption weakness appears priced in. The brokerage favors defensive names with clear earnings visibility like Berli Jucker and CP All. It has add ratings on both and target price of THB26.00 on Berli Jucker and THB65.25 on CP All.(ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0729 GMT - Aker BP's second-quarter production was slightly light, RBC Capital Markets analysts write. The 415,000 barrels of oil equivalent a day was slightly below the 420,000 barrels the analysts had expected. However, it was in line with the company's full-year production guidance of 390,000 to 420,000 barrels a day, they write. Shares fall 2.5% to 260.9 Norwegian krone. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0723 GMT - European airlines shares fell Friday morning on the second day of a strike of France's air-traffic controllers, which is forcing carriers to cancel hundreds of flights. Air France-KLM is down 0.3%, Wizz Air falls 1.4%, EasyJet dips 0.8%, Ryanair and TUI go down 0.7%, and Deutsche Lufthansa loses 0.4%. In London, International Consolidated Airlines Group--comprising British Airways, Iberia and Vueling among others--is flat. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0720 GMT - Air France-KLM's decision to increase its stake in Swedish flag carrier SAS was largely expected at some point, JPMorgan analyst Harry Gowers says. The deal is relatively small but there are some reasons to be positive about it, he says. Air France-KLM will gain greater access to wealthy Scandinavia, and will be able to benefit from cost synergies as SAS becomes a subsidiary of the group, Gowers says. Financial details are light at this stage, he adds. Shares in Air France-KLM are down 0.4%, dragged by the impact of the second day of France's air-traffic controller strike, which has forced airlines to cancel hundreds of flights. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0710 GMT - Rheinmetall's second-quarter growth will be hindered by January's explosion at its facility in Murcia province, southeast Spain, which affected shipments, Jefferies analysts Chloe Lemarie and Ben Brown say. The German arms maker told a second-quarter pre-close call on Thursday evening that it expects a 200 million-euro headwind from the fire. However, the products are almost ready to be shipped and the impact should be fully recovered by the end of the year, starting in the third quarter, the analysts say. Second-quarter growth was also affected by limited order intake from the German government as it was undergoing its post-election transition, they note. Rheinmetall remains on track to meet its fiscal 2025 guidance, they add. Shares closed at 1,698.50 euros on Thursday.(cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0914 GMT - Antofagasta and Anglo American fall as LME three-month copper prices slide 1% to $9,854.0 a metric ton on holiday-thinned trading. In recent weeks, prices have risen on month on persistent U.S. tariff uncertainties. Antofagasta slips 2.1% to 1,893 pence but remains up 3.6% on week and 34% over the past three months. Anglo American falls 2% to 2,198 pence. It remains up 2.2% on week and 22% over the past three months. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0749 GMT - Oil prices are ticking lower in early trade as investors brace for OPEC+'s meeting this weekend, with expectations for another large output hike already factored in. Brent crude slips 0.2% to $68.68 a barrel, while WTI is flat at $66.99 a barrel. Eight key members of the group are anticipated to approve a fourth production increase of 411,000 barrels a day for August--a move that would increase prospects of a supply glut. Traders are also closely monitoring U.S.-Iran nuclear talks after Axios reported that discussions are set to resume next week in Oslo. Meanwhile, Iran's Foreign Minister, Abbas Araghchi, said the country remains committed to the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty and its safeguards agreement following Tehran's decision to suspend cooperation with the U.N. nuclear watchdog. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0729 GMT - Aker BP's second-quarter production was slightly light, RBC Capital Markets analysts write. The 415,000 barrels of oil equivalent a day was slightly below the 420,000 barrels the analysts had expected. However, it was in line with the company's full-year production guidance of 390,000 to 420,000 barrels a day, they write. Shares fall 2.5% to 260.9 Norwegian krone. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: European Midday Briefing: Shares Fall as Trump Tariff Deadline Looms
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Stocks:

European stocks fell Friday as Trump's July 9 tariff deadline loomed ever larger.

If negotiators cannot reach a deal by Wednesday , U.S. tariffs could rise sharply and could prompt tit-for-tat measures.

Amid the risk there may not be agreements, the U.S. could extend negotiations on reciprocal tariffs, ANZ, said.

The U.S.-Vietnam trade deal has sent a strong signal tariffs could be higher than the universal 10% mark, Swissquote Bank said.

Trade and tariffs remained the key uncertainty for eurozone rates markets , according to Societe Generale.

In Germany, May factory orders declined but international demand and orders from outside the eurozone rose, suggesting U.S. firms continued to stockpile goods.

The air traffic control strike in France continued and weighed on aviation shares .

Stocks to Watch

Rheinmetall's second-quarter growth will reflect January's explosion at its facility in southeast Spain, and limited order intake from the German government, according to Jefferies.

U.S. Markets:

Markets are closed today for the country's July 4 holiday after payrolls rose more than expected Thursday.

Forex:

The dollar fell against a basket of currencies after it reached a one-week high Thursday.

The currency could benefit in the short term from Trump's tax and spending bill, but the legislation could have negative implications [https://The dollar could benefit in the short term from President Trump's tax and spending bill, but the legislation could have negative implications for the dollar in the long term, Commerzbank said. https://newsplus.wsj.com/article/realtime/DNCO20250704001310] in the long term, Commerzbank said.

Sterling rose against the dollar but fell against the euro and it faced further losses as markets were yet to factor in the country's fiscal risks, Monex Europe said.

Bonds:

The eurozone bond supply is set to slow down, potentially helping yield spreads to tighten, Societe Generale said, adding this could be supportive for Italian and Spanish bonds.

The 10-year Bund yield was forecast to continue trading in the 2.40%-2.80% range until the end of the year.

The 10-year Treasury yield is expected to stay in the 4.0%-4.5% range in the coming months, Societe Generale said separately.

"While we expect a modest increase in term premia, the rise in long-end yields is likely capped and hence the steepening momentum is likely to wane."

Energy:

Oil prices fell as investors braced for OPEC+'s meeting this weekend and expected another large output hike.

Metals:

Gold futures rose and were on track for a weekly gain of around 2%.

EMEA HEADLINES

Italy's BPER Banca Bumps Popolare Di Sondrio Offer to $6.4 Billion

Italian lender BPER Banca topped up its offer for Banca Popolare di Sondrio by throwing in a cash component to value the smaller rival at around 5.47 billion euros ($6.43 billion).

In February, BPER Banca rushed to join the wave of consolidation sweeping through Italy's crowded banking sector in a bid to gain scale and defend its market position. The offer it put forward for BP Sondrio valued it at roughly 4.3 billion euros based on share prices at the time.

French Regulators Fine Shein for Misleading Discounts

French regulators fined fast-fashion retailer Shein more than $40 million for what they deemed to be misleading practices on prices and environmental claims.

After an 11-month investigation by France's consumer watchdog and its national investigation service, regulators said that Infinite Style E-Commerce, responsible for Shein's products in France, had misled consumers regarding discounts.

Putin Plots Summer of Relentless Attacks on Ukraine

KYIV, Ukraine-As President Trump is pulling back in Ukraine, Russian President Vladimir Putin is pushing forward.

Moscow is ramping up its ground offensives and bombing campaigns against cities across Ukraine as its invasion enters a fourth summer. At the same time, the U.S. decision this week to stop delivery of some weapons to the Ukrainians hands Putin a significant boost to his efforts to weaken Western support that is central to sustaining Kyiv's resistance.

No More Tears. U. K. Finance Minister's Job Appears Safe.

No more tears, a stronger pound, and lower borrowing costs. That is where things stand on Thursday, after the British prime minister made it clear that he supports his finance minister.

On Wednesday, the currency sank and prices of gilts fell, sending yields higher, after Prime Minister Keir Starmer appeared not to back Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves during Parliament's weekly debating session, the Prime Minister's Questions. Reeves wiped away tears, televised images showed.

GLOBAL NEWS

Wall Street Worries as Crisis-Level Deficits Become the Government's Default Mode

U.S. budget deficits were already approaching $2 trillion when Republican lawmakers set out to extend and expand tax cuts this year. Interest rates were high and the bond market was jumpy, producing worrying spikes in borrowing costs.

Republicans forged ahead anyway, defying warnings from Wall Street to Washington that they were pushing the country further down a dangerous fiscal path.

China, U.S. Speeding Up Trade Truce, Chinese Commerce Ministry Says

Beijing and Washington are speeding up the implementation of the trade framework reached in London, China's commerce ministry said Friday.

American officials have informed China that they are lifting a series of restrictive measures on Chinese goods, a ministry spokesperson said.

House Passes Trump's Megabill in GOP Triumph

WASHINGTON-The House narrowly passed Republicans' sprawling tax-and-spending bill, delivering a major second-term victory for President Trump and again demonstrating his power to keep GOP lawmakers in line.

The 218-214 vote Thursday sends the measure to Trump's desk, ahead of the July 4 target he set for Congress to finish the legislation that cuts taxes, boosts border security and lowers social safety-net spending. Trump and GOP leaders muscled the bill through the House after an all-night session, despite many lawmakers' misgivings about the version the Senate passed 51-50 earlier this week.

North Korean, in Rare Feat, Flees to Freedom On Foot Through the DMZ

SEOUL-A North Korean man made a daring escape to freedom, traversing on foot one of the world's most fortified places: the Korean demilitarized zone.

He was detected by South Korea's military around 3 a.m. Thursday, according to Seoul's Defense Ministry, navigating through the land-mine-laden DMZ, which stretches more than 2 miles wide and roughly 150 miles long.

The Yellow Sea Is the New Flashpoint for China's Regional Power Play

SEOUL-One day in February, a South Korean research vessel approached a set of unusual structures planted by China in the middle of the Yellow Sea, the narrow body of water that separates the two countries.

Two large Chinese Coast Guard ships and three Chinese boats quickly blocked the South Korean ship's path. The passengers on the small Chinese boats wielded knives to force the Koreans back.

Write to pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Audi's U.S. Meltdown: 6 Straight Quarters of Decline--But a Radical Pivot May Be Coming
data.description:
Audi, the premium brand under Volkswagen (VWAGY), just posted its sixth consecutive quarterly sales drop in the U.S.a 19% decline in Q2 deliveries, with the top-selling Q5 SUV falling off a cliff at -29%. And yet, in a surprising move, Audi isn't raising prices in July. Instead, it's eating the margin pressure, likely to avoid losing even more ground in a market that's becoming increasingly brutal for German automakers.

The pain isn't just cyclicalit's structural. Audi, like Porsche, still doesn't build any cars in the U.S. Its models are imported from Europe and Mexico, leaving them vulnerable to tariffsa legacy of Trump-era trade policy that continues to bite. And with Chinese EV giants like BYD (BYDDF) undercutting prices in their home market and stealing market share, Audi now finds itself squeezed on both ends: fading in China and flailing in the U.S.

That might explain why Audi's CFO Jurgen Rittersberger is finally signaling a shift. In May, the company said it would pick a U.S. production site this year, and it's reportedly exploring synergies with VW's Scout brand and the existing Tennessee plant. If Audi pulls the trigger, it could be a long-overdue hedge against trade riskand possibly a lifeline in what's shaping up to be a make-or-break U.S. strategy.

data.title: Timee Strengthens Worker Protections with New Labor Contract Policy Effective September
data.description:
Timee will update its service policy effective Sept. 1, aligning with the Spot Work Association's guidelines to establish labor contracts with termination rights when workers apply for jobs, replacing the previous QR code check-in system.

The changes aim to enhance worker protections, including coverage for commuting accidents under workers' compensation and stricter rules on employer cancellations, according to its Tokyo bourse filing on Friday.

Employers can no longer terminate contracts within 24 hours of work start times without penalties, and worker cancellation penalties will now apply 24 hours prior, down from 48 hours previously.

The policy shift follows collaboration with Japan's Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW) to promote compliance and sustainable industry growth while ensuring fair labor practices.

Timee emphasizes its commitment to safe job matching and will continue refining its platform to balance worker rights with employer needs, with minimal expected financial impact for fiscal 2025.

Yes - Primary Health Properties plc

2.         DEALINGS BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

(a)  Purchases and sales

Class of relevant security

Purchases/ Sales

Total number of securities

Highest price per unit paid/received (GBX)

Lowest price per unit paid/received (GBX)

(b)        Cash-settled derivative transactions

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. CFD

  • Nature of dealing

    e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position

  • Number of reference securities

  • Price per unit

(c)        Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)

(i)         Writing, selling, purchasing or varying

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description e.g. call option

  • Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.

  • Number of securities to which option relates

  • Exercise price per unit

  • Type

    e.g. American, European etc.

  • Expiry date

  • Option money paid/ received per unit

(ii)        Exercise

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. call option

  • Exercising/ exercised against

  • Number of securities

  • Exercise price per unit

(d)        Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

  • Class of relevant security

  • Nature of dealing

    e.g. subscription, conversion

  • Details

  • Price per unit (if applicable)

3.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

04/07/2025

data.title: Ghana Cocoa Supply Weigh on Prices, But Weather Poses Risks — Market Talk
data.description: Prospects of improved cocoa supplies from Ghana, the world's second-largest grower of the key chocolate ingredient, have weighed on prices over the past two weeks. However, volatile weather continues to pose downside risks, says market intelligence firm CocoaRadar. The anticipated 8% rise in Ghana's 2025-2026 crop production to 650,000 metric tons signals a recovery, although the market remains caught between improving supply and persistent threats from erratic rainfall and the impact of the El Niño weather phenomenon. "We are at a market inflection point. Signals of supply recovery in Ghana and Nigeria coexist with climate instability and weak farmer incentives. For now, this correction is healthy-but volatility is far from over," CocoaRadar says. (Nicholas.Bariyo@wsj.com;@Nicholasbariyo)

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 AM ET: U.S. Dollar Eases as Tariff Deadline Looms | Wall ...
data.description:
U.S. Dollar Eases as Tariff Deadline Looms

Investors' attention turns to the U.S. 's fiscal deficit and the end of a 90-day pause on so-called U.S. reciprocal tariffs due on July 9.

----

Wall Street Worries as Crisis-Level Deficits Become the Government's Default Mode

New legislation could push federal debt into dangerous territory-without an emergency requiring it.

----

Food Prices Rise Driven by Meat, Vegetable-Oil

Food prices rose in June as higher meat, vegetable-oil and dairy prices offset lower cereal and sugar prices, the Food and Agriculture Organization of the UN said.

----

German Factory Orders Sink as Uncertainty Weighs

The fall in demand was dragged by domestic orders, which dropped 7.8%, while foreign orders held up.

----

China Accelerating Implementation of U.S. Trade Framework, Ministry Says

China is reviewing and approving applications for exports of controlled items to the U.S., as Beijing and Washington speed up the implementation of their trade framework, the Chinese commerce ministry said.

----

House Passes Trump's Megabill in GOP Triumph

The narrow vote delivered the president a major second-term legislative victory.

----

Trump Says He Is 'Very Disappointed' With Putin Conversation on Ukraine

He spoke to the Russian president about the war in Ukraine but made no headway on halting the fighting.

----

Canada Goods Trade Deficit Narrows as Flows With U.S. Continue to Slacken

Helped by volatile gold shipments, the May deficit was down from a record in April and continued to reflect fluctuating tariff policy in Canada's biggest trade partner and neighbor.

----

Want to Know Where the Economy Is Heading? It's All About Earnings.

June jobs data handily beat expectations. But the report was full of asterisks. What matters is tariffs, inflation, and corporate profits.

----

Summer Road Trips to Drive Record Ethanol Use

A record number of drivers are hitting the road this summer, giving the U.S. ethanol industry a needed shot in the arm.

----

June Jobs Report Likely to Extend Fed's Summer Pause

Fed officials have cited solid labor-market readings to justify a wait-and-see stance.

----

Steady Hiring Added 147,000 Jobs to U.S. Economy in June

The jobs report showed manufacturers are pausing hiring amid tariff uncertainty, while unemployment fell partly because fewer people are looking for work.

----

Biggest part of the U.S. economy rebounds from trade-war blues. But it's still weak.

The large service side of the economy rebounded in June after a brief slump in the prior month when U.S. trade wars were at their zenith and companies were paralyzed by indecision.

----

The Dow and Russell 2000 are joining the stock market's party. Is it a game changer for the bulls?

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite tallied fresh record closing highs on Thursday, buoyed by a stronger-than-expected jobs report that helped dampen expectations for a Federal Reserve interest-rate cut in July.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 5 AM ET: TSMC to Delay Japan Chip Plant and Prioritize U.S. to Avoid Trump Tariffs | The ...
data.description:
TSMC to Delay Japan Chip Plant and Prioritize U.S. to Avoid Trump Tariffs

The Taiwanese semiconductor maker is pouring funds more quickly into U.S. expansion.

----

The Corporate Winners and Losers in Trump's Big Tax Bill

Oil drillers win access to public lands and manufacturers get tax breaks, while elite universities face new endowment taxes and solar projects lose tax breaks.

----

These chip-design software stocks are climbing after a U.S.-China trade deal

Siemens, Synopsys and Cadence said the U.S. has lifted export controls restricting sales and business in China.

----

Apollo-Backed Athora Buys U.K. Insurer for $7.8 Billion

The acquisition of Pension Insurance Corp. would effectively turn the specialist insurer into Athora's U.K. insurance business.

----

I Squared Leads Race for KKR's Singapore Logistics Asset

The U.S. private-equity firm has emerged as a leading contender to acquire KKR's Singapore-based supply chain and logistics company, valued at up to $1.5 billion, people familiar with the process said.

----

UPS Offers Buyouts to Drivers, a First in Its 117-Year History

The company is cutting costs because of flat parcel volumes, rising labor costs and a long stock-price slide.

----

Hilco Global to Sell Majority Stake, Launch Direct Lending Unit

The U.S. arm of Japanese investment firm Orix has agreed to acquire 70% of Hilco and commit $1 billion to its new lending business focusing on private credit, sources say.

----

Lilly and Novo Are the Kings of Weight-Loss Drugs. The Companies Coming for Their Crowns.

Both well-established and small, clinical-stage pharma companies are investing heavily to be able to someday compete with Lilly and Novo.

----

Meta Offers to Buy Stake in Venture Funds Started by AI Hires Nat Friedman and Daniel Gross

Limited partners could cash out of a minority of their holdings in venture firm NFDG at current values in a secondary deal with Meta.

----

The Score: Nike, Paramount, Constellation and More Stocks That Defined the Week

Here are some of the major companies whose stocks moved on the week's news.

----

Tesla Stock Diehards Don't Give an Inch

Challenges are piling up for the electric-vehicle maker and CEO Elon Musk, but the company's legion of individual investors are staying put.

----

Thoma Bravo to Take Olo Private in $2 Billion All-Cash Deal

Olo shareholders will receive $10.25 a share, a 65% premium over the share price prior to deal speculation. Shares jumped on the announcement.

----

Gold Reserve Emerges as Top Bidder for Venezuela's Citgo

A court-appointed special master recommended a bid backed by Gold Reserve and Koch over a rival hedge-fund offer.

data.title: Nomura Adjusts Korea Aerospace Industries' Price Target to KRW110,000 From KRW77,000, Keeps at Buy
data.description: Korea Aerospace Industries has an average rating of buy and mean price target of 100,750 South Korean won, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Crude Oil Prices Decline Ahead of Planned Restart of U.S.-Iran Nuclear Talks, OPEC+ Output Hike
data.description:
Crude oil prices fell after Iran reaffirmed its pledge to nuclear non-proliferation and as the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allied producers prepare to boost output.

Brent crude at last look lost 0.6% to US$68.40/barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude fell 0.4% to US$66.71/b on thin trading due to the U.S. Fourth of July holiday.

The U.S. was planning to restart nuclear talks with Iran next week, Axios reported. Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi reportedly said the government remains committed to the nuclear non-proliferation treaty.

"Thursday's news that the U.S. is preparing to resume nuclear talks with Iran, and Araqchi's clarification that cooperation with the U.N. atomic agency has not been halted considerably eases the threat of a fresh outbreak of hostilities," Reuters quoted Vandana Hari, founder of oil market analysis provider Vanda Insights, as saying.

Meanwhile, the OPEC+ producer group is meeting on Sunday and is expected to announce an increase of 411,000 barrels per day of production for August, Reuters reported, citing four delegates from the group.

data.title: Saudi Aramco considers power assets sale to raise billions, sources say
data.description:
Key points:
  • Aramco plans to sell power assets to raise cash, sources say
  • Riyadh presses Aramco to boost profits, sources say
  • Aramco could raise $4 billion from power plants sale, sources say
  • Other asset sales may include port infrastructure, sources say

Saudi oil giant Aramco is looking to sell up to five gas-fired power plants, three sources with knowledge of the matter told Reuters, part of a broader effort to free up funds that could generate tens of billions of dollars.

The potential sale of four or five gas-fired plants that power refineries could alone raise around $4 billion as the Saudi government pushes Aramco to increase profits and payouts to the state, two of the sources said.

Aramco, the world's most profitable company and the main source of Saudi state income, has been looking to sell some assets, improve efficiency and cut costs, Reuters has reported.

The company will also slash dividend payouts by nearly a third this year as lower oil prices hit its income.

The state, which directly owns 81.5% of Aramco, is heavily reliant on the payouts, which include royalties and taxes.

Besides the sale of the gas-fired plants, the company could divest assets such as housing compounds and pipelines, two of the sources said. Port infrastructure assets could also be up for sale, one of them and a third person said.

Aramco declined to comment on the potential asset sales and had no immediate comment on the amount of money the fundraising drive could yield.

The Saudi government communications office did not respond to Reuters requests for comment.

Reuters could not determine a timeline for the sale. The three sources spoke on condition of anonymity because the process is private.

Local businesses like Saudi utility firms could be interested buyers, one of the people said.

Aramco fully or partly owned 18 power plants and related infrastructure locally supplying energy to its gas plants and refineries, according to its 2024 financial report.

Other power plants are expected to come onstream soon. The Tanajib Gas Plant project is expected to start operations this year.

The potential asset sales by Aramco coincide with Saudi Arabia Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's planned massive domestic projects to diversify the economy from oil while facing pressure from tumbling crude prices.

Oil receipts made up 62% of state revenues last year with the Saudi budget showing a deficit of more than $30 billion in 2024 despite a $199 billion windfall from Aramco.

Aramco sold $5 billion of bonds in May and signalled more borrowing.

The country is pouring hundreds of billions of dollars into projects including showpiece events like the Expo 2030 world fair and soccer's FIFA World Cup 2034.

Aramco is also seeking to raise funds for infrastructure by bringing in investors, Reuters reported in May.

FORM 8.5 (EPT/RI)

PUBLIC DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITH RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY STATUS DEALING IN A CLIENT-SERVING CAPACITY

Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")

(a) Name of exempt principal trader:

(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:

     Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree

(c) Name of the party to the offer with which exempt principal trader is connected:

Life Science REIT plc

(d) Date dealing undertaken:

03 Jul 2025

(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to this offer?

     If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"

N/A

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

3.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
